perturbation.original_text: 1. Interpretation 1.1 In this Agreement:- “Agents ” means directors, officers, employees, agents, profes sional advisers, contractors or sub-contractors, or any Group member;
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Interpretation 1.1 In this Agreement:- “Agents ” means only directors and officers of a party, excluding professional advisers, contractors or sub-contractors, or any Group member;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding who qualifies as 'Agents'. The original definition is broad and includes professional advisers, contractors, and subcontractors. The modified definition limits 'Agents' to only directors and officers, excluding those previously included. This creates an internal contradiction if other clauses refer to actions or liabilities of 'Agents' assuming the broader definition, such as clause 2.3(i).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Notwithstanding clause 2.1, the Receiving Party m ay disclose Confidential Information: (i) To those of its Agents who strictly need to know the Confidential Information for the sole purpose set out in clause 2.2 provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Agents are made aware prior to the disclosure of any part of the Confidential Information that the same is confid ential and that they owe a duty of confidence to the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall at all times remain liable for any actions of such Agents that woul d constitute a breach of this Agreement; or (ii) to the extent required by law or the rules of a ny applicable regulatory authority, subject to clause 2.4 below.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Agents' in clause 1.1 significantly narrows the scope from the original, which included 'professional advisers, contractors or sub-contractors, or any Group member'. Clause 2.3(i) refers to 'Agents' who need to know confidential information and holds the Receiving Party liable for their actions. By excluding professional advisers, contractors, and subcontractors from the definition of 'Agents', the modified clause 1.1 creates a contradiction because clause 2.3(i) assumes a broader definition of 'Agents' that now conflicts with the restricted definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described by the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information ” means Information relating to one party (the “Discl osing Party”) and/or the business carried on or proposed or intended to be carried on by that party and which is made available for the purpos es of the Proposal to the other Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of 7 party (the “Receiving Party”) or its Agents by the Discl osing Party or its Agents or recorded in agreed minutes following oral disclosure and any other information otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or it s Agents to the Receiving Party or its Agents, whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement, and whether in writing or otherwise, including any information, anal ysis or specifications derived from, containing or reflecting such information but ex cluding information which:- (i) is publicly available at the time of its disclosu re or becomes publicly available (other than as result of disclosure by the Receiving P arty or any of its Agents contrary to the terms of this letter); or (ii) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Pa rty or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonabl e evidence) free of any restriction as to its use or disclosure prior to its being so disclosed; or (iii) following such disclosure, becomes available to the Receiving Party or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonable evidence) from a source other than the Disclosing Part y or its Agents, which source is not bound by any duty of confidentiality owed, directly or indirectly, to the Disclosing Party in relation to such information;
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information ” means Information relating to one party (the “Discl osing Party”) and/or the business carried on or proposed or intended to be carried on by that party and which is made available for the purpos es of the Proposal to the other Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of 7 party (the “Receiving Party”) or its Agents by the Discl osing Party or its Agents or recorded in agreed minutes following oral disclosure. Confidential Information shall only be considered information explicitly marked and labeled as such. Any other information otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or it s Agents to the Receiving Party or its Agents, whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement, and whether in writing or otherwise, including any information, anal ysis or specifications derived from, containing or reflecting such information but ex cluding information which:- (i) is publicly available at the time of its disclosu re or becomes publicly available (other than as result of disclosure by the Receiving P arty or any of its Agents contrary to the terms of this letter); or (ii) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Pa rty or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonabl e evidence) free of any restriction as to its use or disclosure prior to its being so disclosed; or (iii) following such disclosure, becomes available to the Receiving Party or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonable evidence) from a source other than the Disclosing Part y or its Agents, which source is not bound by any duty of confidentiality owed, directly or indirectly, to the Disclosing Party in relation to such information;
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence requires information to be 'explicitly marked and labeled' as confidential to qualify as 'Confidential Information.' This contradicts the original definition, which includes any information 'made available' regardless of labeling. This introduces an internal contradiction, as the remainder of the clause still refers to 'any other information otherwise made available,' now creating an uncertainty about what exactly is protected under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 In this Agreement:- “Confidential Information ” means Information relating to one party (the “Discl osing Party”) and/or the business carried on or proposed or intended to be carried on by that party and which is made available for the purpos es of the Proposal to the other Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of 7 party (the “Receiving Party”) or its Agents by the Discl osing Party or its Agents or recorded in agreed minutes following oral disclosure. Confidential Information shall only be considered information explicitly marked and labeled as such. Any other information otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or it s Agents to the Receiving Party or its Agents, whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement, and whether in writing or otherwise, including any information, anal ysis or specifications derived from, containing or reflecting such information but ex cluding information which:- (i) is publicly available at the time of its disclosu re or becomes publicly available (other than as result of disclosure by the Receiving P arty or any of its Agents contrary to the terms of this letter); or (ii) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Pa rty or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonabl e evidence) free of any restriction as to its use or disclosure prior to its being so disclosed; or (iii) following such disclosure, becomes available to the Receiving Party or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonable evidence) from a source other than the Disclosing Part y or its Agents, which source is not bound by any duty of confidentiality owed, directly or indirectly, to the Disclosing Party in relation to such information;
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the requirement that 'Confidential Information shall only be considered information explicitly marked and labeled as such.' This directly contradicts the original definition and the subsequent clauses that refer to 'any other information otherwise made available.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Therefore, this introduces a strong in-text contradiction regarding what constitutes Confidential Information, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential and will not, without the Disclosing Par ty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally or in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Receiving Party will endeavor to treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential and will not, without the Disclosing Par ty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally or in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will treat and keep' to 'will endeavor to treat and keep', the obligation on the Receiving Party is weakened. This is contradicting the strict compliance requested in clause 8.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that damages w ould not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it of the provisions of this A greement and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek the remedie s of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for any threat ened or actual breach of any such provision by the Receiving Party or its Agents, an d no proof of special damages shall be necessary for the enforcement of the rights u nder this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party 'will treat and keep' Confidential Information secret, creating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'will endeavor to treat and keep', which implies an attempt rather than a guarantee. This directly contradicts clause 8.1, which states that 'damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach' and that the Disclosing Party is entitled to seek remedies for 'any threatened or actual breach'. The original 'will' aligns with the strict enforcement of clause 8.1, while 'will endeavor' introduces ambiguity and reduces the enforceability of the agreement, thereby creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Duration The obligations of each party and its Agents under t his Agreement shall [be continuing and shall survive the termination of any d iscussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the Proposal continue for a period of 5 (five) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Duration The obligations of each party and its Agents under t his Agreement shall cease after one year from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause stating the agreement lasts for five years after termination of discussions. This creates an internal contradiction between the defined duration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Duration The obligations of each party and its Agents under t his Agreement shall cease after one year from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that obligations continue for five years after termination of discussions. The changed text alters this duration, stating that obligations cease after one year from the date of the agreement. This directly contradicts the originally agreed-upon duration, which as stated in the Perturbation Explanation, creates an internal contradiction between the defined duration of the agreement, as well as the Contradicted Text.
